cases.title: Scenario-matrix row for QR token behavior rejects expired token
preconditions: Student account is authenticated and enrolled in class section
Class session is Open and has rotating QR token with 30-second TTL
steps: Generate a valid token for the open session and advance test clock beyond token expiresAt
Submit POST /v1/check-ins with expired qrToken and idempotency key
Read persisted CheckInAttempt for the request correlation id
cases.expected: Attempt is rejected and persisted with structured outcome code ExpiredQr under AC-04 coverage row.

cases.title: Check-in transaction writes failed attempt for expired or wrong-session token
preconditions: Database has one Open session A and one Open session B with distinct token bindings
Student is enrolled only in session A section
Request correlation and idempotency metadata are enabled
steps: Submit check-in with token bound to session B while student targets session A flow
Submit check-in with an expired token bound to session A
Query CheckInAttempt table and AttendanceRecord table in the same transaction boundary
cases.expected: Each invalid request creates one failed CheckInAttempt with reason code (ExpiredQr or invalid-session equivalent) and creates no successful AttendanceRecord.

cases.title: Lecturer open-close lifecycle gates token validity window
preconditions: Lecturer is assigned to the class section
steps: Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open as Lecturer and capture issued token metadata
Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close as Lecturer
Attempt POST /v1/check-ins using the previously issued token after close
cases.expected: Session transitions Scheduled->Open->Closed are accepted, and post-close check-in using stale token is rejected with structured failure without new success record.

cases.title: Parallel duplicate submissions preserve single success invariant
preconditions: Student has one valid token for an Open session
No existing successful attendance record exists for this student-session pair
steps: Send two concurrent POST /v1/check-ins requests for same student and same token with different idempotency keys
Collect both API outcomes and inspect persisted attendance records
cases.expected: At most one request succeeds and any duplicate path returns DuplicateCheckIn conflict, preserving one success per student-session.

cases.title: POST /v1/check-ins returns 422 envelope with ExpiredQr for expired token
preconditions: Open session exists with token that can be expired deterministically
steps: Let issued QR token exceed 30-second TTL
Call POST /v1/check-ins with expired qrToken and required headers
Validate HTTP status, envelope shape (data/meta/error), and error.code
cases.expected: API returns 422 with error.code ExpiredQr and no restricted or inconsistent payload leakage.

cases.title: Wrong-session token is rejected with invalid-session equivalent reason
preconditions: Two active sessions exist in different sections with distinct QR tokens
Student is authenticated in a check-in flow for session A
steps: Submit POST /v1/check-ins using session B token while attempting session A attendance
Read response error payload and check-in attempt record from audit trail
cases.expected: Request is rejected with structured invalid-session reason and logged as failed attempt; no successful attendance mutation is created.

cases.title: Out-of-scope roles are denied report and export APIs without data leakage
preconditions: Student and ITAdmin identities are available without explicit academic export grants
Attendance data exists in multiple sections
steps: Call GET /v1/reports/attendance as Student for non-self scope
Call POST /v1/exports/attendance as Student and ITAdmin for academic dataset
Verify status and body contain denial code and no partial restricted data
cases.expected: Unauthorized role/scope calls are denied (403 Forbidden/OutOfScope) and return no cross-scope report or export content.

cases.title: Student browser flow shows expired-token error and allows retry path
preconditions: Student is logged in on mobile-web browser
Lecturer opened session and QR is displayed with rotating TTL
steps: Student scans token after TTL expiry and submits check-in form
Observe inline error reason displayed for expired token
Student rescans fresh QR token and re-submits
cases.expected: UI surfaces structured expired-token failure feedback for first attempt and permits retry with fresh token without breaking session flow.
